title: Put scout behind the shared Caddy/SSO edge
userStory: As the user, I want scout deployed behind the shared edge so that it is reachable over HTTPS at scout.{domain}, gated by the same Google sign-in as the other apps, and installable as a PWA — without scout holding any auth code.
acceptanceCriteria: Scout gets a compose service on the brain's internal network (no public port), alongside brain/pwa
A Caddy vhost scout.{domain} → forward_auth oauth2-proxy → scout, following the US-001 edge template from app-platform.md and the existing compose/Caddyfile pattern
The owner's email is covered by the oauth2-proxy whitelist; scout reads identity from the injected X-Auth-Request-Email header and exposes /api/me; scout itself contains no login flow
Scout is registered in the launcher app-registry (brainbot FEAT-...-4fd1 US-004) with its health URL
[Verification] From a browser: hitting scout.{domain} unauthenticated bounces through Google sign-in, then loads scout over HTTPS; /api/me returns the signed-in email; the PWA installs to home screen
externalDependencies: brainbot FEAT-20260607_155517-4fd1 US-001 (edge template) and US-004 (launcher registry)
